page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

The x86_64 architecture of the AMD EPYC processor supports several hardware-level instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), SHA Extensions (SHA_NI), Advanced Encryption Standard (AES), and Bit Manipulation Instruction Set 2 (BMI2). However, it lacks support for AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The CPU topology utilizes single-threaded physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, supported by a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1 data cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache.

Benchmark evaluations indicate a mixed performance profile. The instance demonstrates average performance in Geekbench single-core (1315.0) and multi-core (5025.0) tests, as well as in Passmark CPU (10249.39) and memory (2067.12) evaluations. It handles static web serving at 6.78 GB/sec and Redis SET operations at 7.44 million operations per second. However, raw computational throughput is limited, as shown by weak multi-core stress-ng results and lower uncached memory write bandwidth of 16.75 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated core allocation offers predictable performance for compute-bound workloads without the overhead of shared-core resource contention. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average performance across text processing, software compilation, and database operations, this instance is suited for moderate web serving, application servers, build systems, and distributed microservices that do not require massive memory footprints or local high-speed storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7H12 processor running at 2.6 GHz. Built on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a dedicated core allocation with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for tasks like software compilation, database operations, and static web serving, though raw multi-core stress testing and uncached memory bandwidth show weaker results. Supported instruction sets include AVX, AES, and SHA_NI, while AVX-512 is absent. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without high memory demands, making it suitable for web serving, microservices, and moderate build pipelines.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, offering balanced mid-tier performance for web serving and microservices.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6a.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and KVM virtualization.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core compute instance designed for moderate web serving, application hosting, and balanced multi-threaded processing workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC 7H12 vCPUs at 2.6 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Supports AVX, AES, and SHA_NI instruction sets
Suited for web serving, microservices, and software compilation
